title: Relational Storage And Migrations And RLS Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the relational storage and migrations and rls capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Governs the canonical relational store as a forward-only, numbered migration chain applied by a deterministic runner inside one transaction per file, recording each applied file with its content checksum so already-applied files become effectively immutable and corrections ship only as new forward migrations (never edits). Fresh installs apply the full chain in code-point order; an existing install skips checksum-matched files and applies only the new tail, so an existing install is already a current install at the data layer with zero data movement for prior files. The runner refuses to proceed on any checksum mismatch for an applied file, making the chain the load-bearing provenance and the data-verification gate.

stateModel.description: State is held in a single canonical relational database with two tenant-isolation layers: a schema-per-tenant model where per-tenant configuration lives in tenant-scoped schemas selected by a transaction-local search path, and public-schema tenant-scoped tables carrying a tenant/organisation identifier column protected by row-level security. The migration ledger table tracks each applied file name (unique), its checksum, and apply timestamp; checksum is the first 16 hex characters of the SHA-256 of the committed file bytes, computed over the file with secret placeholders intact so it is identical across every environment. Tenant-scoped tables ENABLE and FORCE row-level security; provider substrates such as cache, search, and analytics are adapters behind ports and never own canonical schema.

permissions.description: The runtime application connects as a non-superuser role that is explicitly NOINHERIT and does not bypass row-level security, so tenant isolation is enforced for normal traffic; privileged operations switch transaction-locally to a dedicated bypass role. The canonical RLS predicate admits a row only when its tenant identifier equals the transaction-local current-tenant setting, OR when the current role is the bypass role or inherits bypass membership while role inheritance is active; the naive membership-only form is forbidden because it leaked cross-tenant rows in an earlier table and was corrected by a forward migration. Superuser and inheriting administrative roles bypass RLS by design and are used only for migration, seed, and system-admin paths.

contracts.description: No client connects directly to the store; all data access is mediated through repository ports and adapters per the hexagonal constraint, with each bounded context exposing typed repository operations. The migration contract is forward-only with one transaction per file, code-point apply ordering, and a single secret-materialisation trust boundary that substitutes only the managed application-role password placeholder after rejecting quote, backslash, and control characters. New tenant-scoped tables MUST attach the canonical RLS predicate and the FORCE row-level-security setting as a contract obligation.

validation.description: The runner validates that every previously applied file still hashes to its recorded checksum on each run, refusing to advance on drift, and validates that the database has been migrated before any seed step executes. Identity and reference invariants are enforced in schema (uniqueness on natural keys, role and status check constraints, foreign keys), and the secret placeholder is validated by a trust-boundary check before materialisation. Tenant-identifier presence is a precondition for tenant-scoped access because RLS denies rows when the current-tenant setting is absent.

errorModel.description: Failures are deterministic and fail-closed: a checksum mismatch or a failing migration aborts the run, rolls back that file's transaction, and records nothing, so the operator fixes forward and re-runs safely. Missing or invalid configuration fails closed rather than inventing defaults, and provider failure returns a typed degraded or readiness error while keeping mutation boundaries closed and never fabricating successful provider state. Tenant data is never silently dropped or remapped; rollback is forward-fix or a guarded restore only after migration lineage is verified.

auditModel.description: State-changing access preserves tenant context through route, use-case, and provider spans, and typed error logs carry the tenant/organisation identifier when the operation is tenant-scoped. The migration ledger is itself an audit trail: each applied file is recorded with its checksum and timestamp, and historical files retain their decision-reference comment blocks so that later forward corrections remain traceable to the file they supersede. Recovery and restore must preserve tenant boundary, audit, and data-subject evidence.

readinessModel.description: Readiness depends on the substrate being reachable and fully migrated; the migrated check (a non-empty migration ledger) and the cross-tenant isolation live-proof (an unfiltered count under a foreign tenant context returning zero) are the acceptance gates. When the substrate is unavailable the capability degrades explicitly, disabling mutation and side-effect paths while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible rather than failing silently. Promotion is blocked when readiness is unavailable or when the isolation or audit signal is missing.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by repository substrate tests and live isolation proofs that assert RLS denies cross-tenant reads and that the checksum and migrated-state gates hold; these caught a real naive-predicate leak before promotion. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
